Globalize template added. The "In Culture" section is very much western only, which surprised me a lot. Deserts are present in a bunch of eastern religions and cultures (Islam, arabic and persian literatures, etc), as well as in western ones (Christianity), and none of this is reflected in said section of the article. I don't quite get it. Neither is there a link to some other article talking about it which could justify the lack of it in this one.

A '''desert''' is a [[landscape]] or [[region]] of land that is very dry because of low rainfall amounts ([[precipitation]]), often has little coverage by plants, and in which streams dry up unless they are supplied by water from outside areas.

A '''desert'' is a [[landscape]] or [[region]] of land that is very dry, often has little coverage by plants, and in which streams dry up unless they are supplied by water from outside areas."<ref name=EG>Essentials of Geology, p452, 3rd Ed, Stephen Marsh
